content.problemStatement:
Christmas is celebrated on 25th25^{th}25th December every year.

You are given a day XXX in December. Determine whether it is Christmas.

Print `CHRISTMAS` if it is Christmas. Otherwise print `ORDINARY`.


Input Format

The input consists of a single integer XXX, denoting a day in December.


Output Format
Output on a new line `CHRISTMAS`, if it is Christmas and `ORDINARY` otherwise.


You may print each character in uppercase or lowercase. For example the strings `CHRISTMAS`, `christmas`, `Christmas` are all considered identical.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (1 ≤ X ≤ 31), the day in December.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line "CHRISTMAS" if X equals 25, otherwise output "ORDINARY".

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: X=25, which is Christmas, so output CHRISTMAS. Second test case: X=12, not Christmas, so output ORDINARY.
X=1 and X=31 are ordinary days, X=25 is Christmas.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. For each test case read X and compare it with 25. If equal, print CHRISTMAS; otherwise print ORDINARY. This is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time comparison per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
